questions.question: Explain the trial-and-error learning process in Reinforcement Learning with an example.
keyPoints: Definition of trial-and-error learning in RL context
Description of the feedback loop between agent and environment
Example (e.g., robot navigating a maze, game playing)
Explanation of how agent improves policy based on rewards

questions.question: What is the exploration-exploitation dilemma? Discuss at least two strategies to balance them.
keyPoints: Explanation of why it matters in RL
At least two strategies: epsilon-greedy, softmax, UCB, Thompson Sampling

questions.question: Define the following terms: Agent, Environment, Reward, Policy, Discount Factor.
keyPoints: Agent: The learning entity that takes actions
Environment: The external system the agent interacts with
Reward: Feedback signal indicating immediate desirability
Policy: Mapping from states to actions
Discount Factor: Parameter that weights immediate vs future rewards

questions.question: How does Reinforcement Learning differ from Supervised and Unsupervised Learning?
keyPoints: RL learns from feedback (rewards/penalties), supervised from labels, unsupervised from data patterns
RL doesn't require labeled data or pre-defined patterns
RL agent interacts with environment directly
